info.name: Voise Ad Sim — E2E Flows
info.description:
Step-by-step tests for the Voise ad server, using the simulator and a mock ad buyer (DSP).

**How to run:** open folders 0 → 5 in order and click Send on each request.

**Before folder 3:** run folder 1 first — it saves a real ad tag ID into {{tag_id}}.

**Tip:** keep the simulator backend terminal open during folder 3. You will see each auction request and whether the mock buyer bid.

**Variables** (Collection → Variables tab):
- sim_base = simulator (default http://localhost:8090)
- adserver_base = Voise ad server (default http://localhost:8001)
- tag_id = filled automatically by folder 1
- publisher_id = 1192 (matches the team CTV sample)
- request_id = set automatically before each auction request

item.name: 0 - Check everything is running
item.description: Quick checks that the simulator, ad server, and mock buyer are up before you run any tests.

item.name: Check simulator and ad server are up

request.description:
Checks that the simulator is running and can reach the Voise ad server.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 and `"ad_server_reachable": true`.

**If it fails:** start the ad server on port 8001 (or update the `adserver_base` variable).

item.name: See which ad server URLs the simulator found

request.description:
Shows the live API routes the simulator discovered on the ad server (VAST, OpenRTB, ads.txt, etc.).

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with a list of resolved endpoints.

**When to use:** optional sanity check — useful if routes changed or ports differ.

Shows how the mock ad buyer (DSP) is configured and how many bids it has placed.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with `mode` (bid / no_bid), price settings, and request/bid counters.

**When to use:** before and after folder 3 to confirm the buyer received auction traffic.

item.name: 1 - Set up test data
item.description: Creates advertisers, campaigns, publishers, and registers the mock buyer on the ad server. Required before testing auctions in folder 3.

item.name: Create test advertisers and save ad tag ID

exec: console.log('WARNING: no ad units returned — folder 3 may fail.');

request.description:
Sets up a small test world: advertisers with budget, campaigns, publishers/ad units, and registers the simulator's mock buyer as a demand partner on the ad server.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200. The test script saves the first ad unit's tag ID into `{{tag_id}}` — check the Postman Console for `Saved ad tag ID → ...`.

**You must run this before folder 3.** Without a tag ID, auction requests will fail.

item.name: 2 - Test video ads (VAST)
item.description: Sends many simple video ad requests through VAST and shows fill metrics.

request.description:
Drives 200 VAST video ad requests at the ad server and fires impression + quartile tracking pixels.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with a `run_id`. Traffic runs in the background — watch the simulator dashboard for progress.

**Next step:** use the two requests below to see totals and fill breakdown.

Shows high-level counts: requests sent, responses received, impressions, clicks, and fill rate.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with numeric totals (may be low right after starting folder 2 — wait a few seconds and send again).

item.name: See real ads vs placeholder ads

request.description:
Breaks down responses into real campaign ads, default filler ("Video Ad"), no-fill, and errors.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with counts per category.

**Why it matters:** confirms the ad server is serving real creatives, not only placeholders.

item.name: 3 - Test ad auction (OpenRTB)
item.description: Sends a real CTV OpenRTB bid request (matching the team's sample) to the ad server, runs an auction, and proves the mock buyer controls the outcome.

item.name: Turn mock buyer ON (will place bids)

request.description:
Tells the mock ad buyer (DSP) to participate in auctions and submit bids.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200. The next auction request should return a winning ad.

**Run this before:** "Send ad auction request — expect an ad back".

item.name: Send ad auction request — expect an ad back

exec: if (code === 204) { console.log('204 — no ad returned (no-bid).'); pm.test('got a response', function(){ return true; }); return; }
var j; try { j = pm.response.json(); } catch (e) { console.log('empty or non-JSON body'); return; }
var hasBid = j.seatbid && j.seatbid.length && j.seatbid[0].bid && j.seatbid[0].bid.length;
console.log('Ad returned — seat=' + j.seatbid[0].seat + '  price=' + bid.price + '  creative=' + bid.crid);
pm.test('HTTP 200 with a winning bid', function () { pm.expect(code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('bid has seat, price > 0, and VAST creative', function () {
pm.test('Expected an ad — is the mock buyer ON and seed done?', function () { pm.expect(hasBid).to.be.ok; });

request.description:
Sends a CTV OpenRTB bid request to the Voise ad server — same shape as the team's inbound sample (Samsung Tizen TV, Philo app, 1920×1080 video, publisher 1192, supply chain, US privacy).

**What happens:** the ad server runs an auction and calls the simulator's mock buyer. Watch the simulator terminal for `MOCK DSP` and `DECISION: BID`.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with a winning bid (`seatbid`), a price above the floor (5.775), and a VAST creative in `adm`. The Console prints `Ad returned — seat=...`.

**Important:**
- Run folder 1 first so `{{tag_id}}` is set.
- First call can take up to ~30 seconds (cold start). Set Postman timeout to 60000 ms (Settings → General).
- `request_id` is generated automatically so `id` and `source.ext.schain.nodes[0].rid` always match.

item.name: Check mock buyer received the request

request.description:
Confirms the mock buyer was called during the last auction.

**What to expect:** the `bids` counter increased and `last_response` shows the buyer's decision (BID).

**When to use:** right after "Send ad auction request — expect an ad back".

item.name: Turn mock buyer OFF (will not bid)

request.description:
Tells the mock buyer to refuse all auctions (no-bid).

**What to expect:** HTTP 200. The next identical auction request should return no ad.

**Why:** proves the auction outcome really depends on the buyer, not a fixed response.

item.name: Send same auction request — expect no ad

exec: if (code === 204) { console.log('204 no-bid — correct (mock buyer is OFF).'); pm.test('no ad when buyer is OFF', function(){ return true; }); return; }
var j; try { j = pm.response.json(); } catch (e) { console.log('empty body — also OK for no-bid'); return; }
var hasBid = j.seatbid && j.seatbid.length && j.seatbid[0].bid && j.seatbid[0].bid.length;
console.log(hasBid ? 'Unexpected ad returned!' : 'No ad (nbr=' + j.nbr + ') — correct.');
pm.test('no winning bid when mock buyer is OFF', function () { pm.expect(!!hasBid).to.eql(false); });

request.description:
Sends the same CTV OpenRTB request as the previous step, but the mock buyer is now set to no-bid.

**What to expect:** HTTP 204 or a response with no winning bid. The simulator terminal should show `DECISION: NO-BID`.

**Why:** confirms the ad server auction actually calls the buyer and respects a no-bid.

Restores the mock buyer to normal bidding mode after the no-bid test.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with `mode: bid`.

item.name: Optional — test mock buyer directly (skip ad server)

request.description:
Optional. Sends a small test bid request straight to the mock buyer, without going through the ad server.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with a bid response, or 204 if the buyer is in no-bid mode.

**When to use:** debugging the buyer alone — not part of the normal end-to-end flow.

item.name: 4 - Run automated checks
item.description: Runs built-in conformance scenarios (S1–S15) against IAB standards.

request.description:
Runs all 15 conformance scenarios in the background (VAST, OpenRTB, privacy, supply chain, etc.).

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 immediately; scenarios finish in 1–2 minutes.

**Next step:** open folder 5 for scorecard and findings.

item.name: Run one check only (change scenario ID)

request.description:
Runs a single scenario instead of all 15.

**Default:** S10 (OpenRTB conformance). Change `"S10"` to S1–S15, or A/B/C/D for grouped runs.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200; result appears in folder 5 or the dashboard.

item.name: 5 - View results
item.description: Read scores, findings, and downloadable reports after running traffic or scenarios.

request.description:
Shows a grade per IAB standard: VAST, VMAP, OpenRTB, ads.txt, supply chain, etc.

**What to expect:** PASS, FAIL, WARN, or GAP per area.

Lists every conformance finding with the spec section, expected value, and what was observed.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with an array of findings (may be empty if nothing failed).

item.name: Compare simulator counts vs ad server

request.description:
Compares what the simulator sent (impressions, clicks) with what the ad server reported, per campaign.

**What to expect:** counts within tolerance = PASS; large drift = investigate tracking or reporting.

Fetches and validates the ad server's ads.txt, app-ads.txt, and sellers.json.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with parse results and any supply-chain findings.

Returns the GAP report as markdown — suitable for sharing with the ad-server team.

**Also saved to:** `backend/reports/GAP_REPORT.md` on the simulator machine.

Returns the same report as JSON (findings, scorecard, scenario verdicts) for scripts or CI.

**What to expect:** HTTP 200 with structured JSON.
